Container.Name: cascade
Container.Overview:
Cascade is a cross-platform cloud storage filesystem client that streams file content on demand and presents multiple backends — Google Drive, S3, local storage, and P2P — as a single virtual tree.
This container runs the Cascade daemon in headless seed mode by default (CASCADE_MOUNT=0): no FUSE mount is attempted, no extra capabilities are required, and the tree is browsable over an in-process WebDAV server at the configured port.
To enable a FUSE-based filesystem mount inside the container (so that the host or sibling containers can access files directly at a path), set CASCADE_MOUNT=1 and switch to Extra Parameters: --cap-add SYS_ADMIN --device /dev/fuse and add the rshared bind-mount for the mount point. See the template config notes and docs/docker.md in the project repository for the full privilege requirements.
For Google Drive: after starting the container for the first time, run the one-time device-code authorisation step from the UnRAID console: docker exec -it cascade cascade --config /config backend auth gdrive Tokens are persisted under /config and refreshed silently on subsequent starts.
Container.Description: Cross-platform cloud storage filesystem daemon. Streams files on demand from Google Drive, S3, local storage, and P2P backends. Default seed mode requires no elevated capabilities; set CASCADE_MOUNT=1 for an in-container FUSE mount (requires --cap-add SYS_ADMIN and --device /dev/fuse).

Variable.Name: CASCADE_BACKEND_TYPE
Variable.Description: Type of the primary backend to provision on first boot. One of: gdrive, s3, local, p2p. Required — the daemon exits cleanly (no work) when no backends are configured. Drives which other CASCADE_* variables are relevant.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_BACKEND_NAME
Variable.Description: Logical name for the backend entry. Becomes the [backends.<name>] key in config.toml and the per-backend <name>.toml filename. Defaults to the value of CASCADE_BACKEND_TYPE when left empty.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_GDRIVE_CLIENT_ID
Variable.Description: Google OAuth2 Desktop client ID. Required when CASCADE_BACKEND_TYPE=gdrive. Obtain from the Google Cloud Console (project credentials, Desktop application type). After first start, run the one-time device-code authorisation: docker exec -it cascade cascade --config /config backend auth gdrive Tokens are persisted under /config/gdrive-tokens/ and refreshed automatically.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_GDRIVE_CLIENT_SECRET
Variable.Description: Google OAuth2 client secret. Required when CASCADE_BACKEND_TYPE=gdrive. Stored in the container's config file (mode 0600), never placed on the command line.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_GDRIVE_ACCOUNT
Variable.Description: Account identifier used for token persistence. Tokens are stored at /config/gdrive-tokens/<account>.json. Defaults to the value of CASCADE_BACKEND_NAME when left empty.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_S3_ENDPOINT
Variable.Description: S3 endpoint URL — e.g. https://s3.amazonaws.com or a MinIO URL. Required when CASCADE_BACKEND_TYPE=s3.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_S3_BUCKET
Variable.Description: S3 bucket name. Required when CASCADE_BACKEND_TYPE=s3.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_S3_REGION
Variable.Description: S3 region. Optional; defaults to us-east-1. Required when CASCADE_BACKEND_TYPE=s3.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID
Variable.Description: S3 access key ID. Required when CASCADE_BACKEND_TYPE=s3. Stored in the per-backend config file, never placed on the command line.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
Variable.Description: S3 secret access key. Required when CASCADE_BACKEND_TYPE=s3. Stored in the per-backend config file (mode 0600), never placed on the command line.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_LOCAL_ROOT
Variable.Description: Filesystem root for a local (adopt-and-sync) backend. Required when CASCADE_BACKEND_TYPE=local. Should be a path under CASCADE_DATA_DIR, e.g. /data/local.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_CONFIG_DIR
Variable.Description: Root config directory the daemon reads. Must be a writable bind-mount or named volume so config.toml, per-backend TOML files, state.db, cascade.pid, and gdrive tokens survive container restarts and updates. Maps to the --config global flag.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_DATA_DIR
Variable.Description: Base directory for bulky runtime state: P2P block store and index, local-backend roots, and cache. Use a separate volume mapped to an UnRAID array share so it can be sized independently of the config volume.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_MOUNT_POINT
Variable.Description: Mount point written to [mount].point in config.toml. In seed mode (CASCADE_MOUNT=0) this path is recorded but nothing is mounted there. In CASCADE_MOUNT=1 mode the FUSE filesystem appears here; with rshared bind propagation it is also visible on the host at the corresponding host path.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_MOUNT
Variable.Description:
Browsable-optional toggle.
0 (default) — seed mode: the entrypoint adds --no-mount and forces the WebDAV presenter. The daemon runs without any FUSE mount; no extra capabilities are required. The tree is accessible over WebDAV at CASCADE_WEBDAV_BIND.
1 — browsable mode: enables the in-container FUSE mount at CASCADE_MOUNT_POINT. REQUIRES all of the following: Extra Parameters: --cap-add SYS_ADMIN --device /dev/fuse A bind-mount of CASCADE_MOUNT_POINT with rshared propagation (add a host path mapping for CASCADE_MOUNT_POINT and set propagation to rshared in the advanced volume settings, or use the compose templates in deploy/). Do not enable this unless you understand the privilege trade-off.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_WEBDAV_BIND
Variable.Description: Bind address for the in-process WebDAV server. Set to 0.0.0.0:<port> to make the tree browsable from the host or other containers without a FUSE mount. Set to 127.0.0.1:8080 to restrict access to the container itself. The mapped port (default 8080) must match the port published in Networking above.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_P2P
Variable.Description: Enables the engine's P2P block-sharing optimisation layer in front of cloud backends. Independent of a pure p2p-type backend — set this to 1 when a cloud-backed node should also fetch blocks from nearby peers (e.g. other Cascade nodes on the same LAN). Maps to [p2p] enabled in config.toml.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_P2P_POSTURE
Variable.Description:
Discovery reach for P2P. One of: lan-only, private, public.
lan-only — UDP multicast on the local network only. private — LAN discovery plus gossip, hole-punching, and relay within a trusted mesh. Nothing is published to any global directory. The default for most nodes. public — Everything in private plus announce-server registration and Mainline DHT. Required for WAN relay (CASCADE_P2P_RELAY_ENDPOINT) to activate. A NAS node that needs to reach peers across the internet should set this.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_P2P_RELAY_ENDPOINT
Variable.Description: host:port of the cascade-relay server used for WAN NAT traversal. Required for the relay path to be usable when CASCADE_P2P_POSTURE=public and the node is behind NAT. Must match CASCADE_RELAY_SHARED_SECRET on the relay side. Example: relay.example.com:9999 See deploy/docker-compose.full.yml for a co-located relay setup.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_P2P_RELAY_SECRET
Variable.Description: 64-character hex HMAC secret authenticating this node to the relay. Must match CASCADE_RELAY_SHARED_SECRET on the cascade-relay container. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32 Stored in the per-backend config file (mode 0600), never placed on the command line.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_P2P_LISTEN
Variable.Description: BEP listener bind address for a p2p-type backend. A bound listener is required for LAN discovery, hole-punch, and DHT/announce self-activation. Set to a fixed port (e.g. 0.0.0.0:22000) when the operator port-forwards or publishes the container port to the host. The mapped port must match the port published in Networking above.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_P2P_ANNOUNCE_URL
Variable.Description: Optional announce-server base URL for serverless peer discovery at posture=public. Example: https://cascade-announce.<your-account>.workers.dev Requires CASCADE_P2P_ANNOUNCE_SECRET. Leave empty if not using an announce server.

Variable.Name: CASCADE_P2P_ANNOUNCE_SECRET
Variable.Description: 64-character hex HMAC write key for the announce server. Required when CASCADE_P2P_ANNOUNCE_URL is set. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32 Stored in the per-backend config file, never placed on the command line.

Variable.Name: RUST_LOG
Variable.Description: Standard tracing log filter for the daemon. Common values: error, warn, info (default), debug, trace. Use debug for initial bring-up diagnosis; revert to info for normal operation to avoid filling the UnRAID log.

Config.Name: Config directory
Config.Description: Config volume — config.toml, per-backend TOML files, state.db, gdrive tokens. Use fast (SSD) storage.

Config.Name: Data directory
Config.Description: Data volume — P2P block store, cache, local-backend roots. Point at an UnRAID array share for bulk storage.

Config.Name: WebDAV port
Config.Description: WebDAV browse port. Browse the virtual tree at http://<host>:<port>/ from any WebDAV-capable client.

Config.Name: P2P listener port
Config.Description: BEP peer listener port. Port-forward this from your router for WAN inbound connections (posture=public). Only needed when CASCADE_P2P=1 or CASCADE_BACKEND_TYPE=p2p.
